content.full_text:
LAW OFFICES
OF

ABRAMSON, CHURCH & STAVE
75 EAST GABILAN STREET

ANDREW CHURCH
JOSEPH A. STAVE
GEORGE E. MCGINNIS
ARNOLD B. MYERS
RICHARD B. ABRAMSON
PAUL D. GUGLION
WILLIAM M. STOFFELS, JR.
DAVID M. CHIESA
YVONNE A ASCHER

SALINAS, CALIFORNIA 93901

TELEPHONE (408) 758-2401

SIDNEY L CHURCH (1908-952)
JACOB ABRAMSON (1909-1972)
JOHN O. STEWART (1920-1981)

FAX (408) 758-2028

November 5, 1990

Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA    95804

Re:    California Iceberg Lettuce Commission

Gentlemen:

The undersigned is the general counsel for the California Iceberg Lettuce Commission and in that capacity I have had the opportunity to review the Commission's Conflict of Interest Code in light of the new statutory changes.

The Conflict of Interest Code provides for business positions; however, does not provide for consultants.  We have reviewed the material which you sent to us regarding consultants and we believe that the Conflict of Interest Code need not be amended at this time; however, we would like confirmation from your office.

The California Iceberg Lettuce Commission was created by statute in order to provide a vehicle to promote iceberg head lettuce in the marketplace.  The Commission has promotions with supermarkets, tie-ins with food manufacturers, such as Kraft, and has some advertising campaigns.  In connection with these activities, the Commission hires a marketing firm that makes surveys of the consumption of iceberg lettuce, as well as the types of promotional materials that seem to appeal to supermarket store managers.  They then report back to the Commission the results of their findings.  The Commission also hires advertising firms to help with the design and dissemination of the marketing materials.  While these people

Fair Political Practices Comision
November 5, 1990
Page 2




are independent contractors, they do not appear to be "consultants"
within the meaning of the regulations.  These firms do not make
any independent decisions but only report information to the Commission and the Commission in turn makes decisions based partly upon
that information.

It would be appreciated if you would review the Conflict of Interest
Code for the California Iceberg Lettuce Commission and indicate
whether or not the Code needs to be amended in any respect.

Your cooperation is greatly appreciated and we look forward to
hearing from you.


Very truly yours,

[signature]
Andrew Church
For ABRAMSON, CHURCH & STAVE

AC/pfs

cc:  Wade Whitfield
     California Iceberg Lettuce Commission

sections.question: The Conflict of Interest Code provides for business positions; however, does not provide for consultants. We have reviewed the material which you sent to us regarding consultants and we believe that the Conflict of Interest Code need not be amended at this time; however, we would like confirmation from your office.
sections.question_synthetic: Does the California Iceberg Lettuce Commission's Conflict of Interest Code need to be amended to address consultants, or are the marketing and advertising firms it engages (which do not make independent decisions) appropriately classified as something other than consultants?
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter, not an FPPC response. The letter contains a question/request but no formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections, as it is correspondence TO the FPPC requesting advice. No FPPC response content is present in this document.

embedding.summary: Incoming letter from counsel for the California Iceberg Lettuce Commission requesting FPPC confirmation regarding whether the Commission's Conflict of Interest Code needs to be amended to account for consultant positions, arguing that the marketing and advertising firms they hire are independent contractors but not "consultants" under regulations.
